questions.question: What are the treatment effectiveness rates for the top departments by patient volume for the "Cured & Discharged" and "Home Recovery" outcomes?

answer.value: The two-step process (auto collapse + calculate conversion rate) clearly reveals the distinct, specialized roles of Internal Medicine and Surgery in driving positive patient outcomes, uncovering patterns that were obscured in the original dense diagram.

key_insights: Collapsing the "Department" layer to the top 2 nodes (Internal Med, 900 pts; Surgery, 550 pts) aggregates the smaller departments (Pediatrics, Cardiology, etc.) into "Others (Layer 1)" (625 pts).
This simplified view immediately shows that Internal Med and Surgery are the dominant care hubs, handling over 70% of all patients.
Internal Medicine has a 68% conversion rate to "Cured & Discharged", making it the most effective department for curative care. This is driven by its strong link to Medication treatment.
Surgery has a 55% conversion rate to "Home Recovery", making it the primary driver of post-operative recovery outcomes.

reasoning.reasoning: We just care about top2 layer
We want to know their treatment effectiveness
